machine.name: localai/medication_adherence
machine.description: CareKit medication adherence classifier. Reads three pre-normalised scalars — medication adherence ratio, task completion ratio, symptom_ok flag — from the Perception Engine and asserts one of four mutually exclusive states: adherent, partial, lapsed, or concern. Structurally parallel to personal_health_baseline: all sequences are isInitial and fire in OR arbitration; the five guards partition the [med × task × symptom] space with full coverage and no overlap.

metadata.author: localAIStack health bridge

localai_carekit_med_adherence.description: Medication adherence ratio (0.0–1.0): doses taken / doses scheduled in rolling 24 h window

localai_carekit_task_completion.description: CareKit task completion ratio (0.0–1.0): tasks completed / tasks scheduled today

localai_carekit_symptom_ok.description: Symptom sentinel: 1.0 = no symptoms or severity < moderate; 0.0 = moderate or worse symptoms reported

stateLogic.adherent: med ≥ 0.5 AND task ≥ 0.5 AND symptom ≥ 0.5 — full compliance, no symptoms
stateLogic.partial-task: med ≥ 0.5 AND task < 0.5 — medication taken but task completion lagging (symptom wildcard)
stateLogic.partial-symptom: med ≥ 0.5 AND task ≥ 0.5 AND symptom < 0.5 — medication and tasks done but symptoms flagged
stateLogic.lapsed: med < 0.5 AND symptom ≥ 0.5 — missed medication but no symptom escalation
stateLogic.concern: med < 0.5 AND symptom < 0.5 — missed medication AND symptoms present
stateLogic.mutualExclusivity: adherent ⊃ partial-* ⊃ lapsed ⊃ concern; all five guards partition [med×task×symptom] with no overlap; partial-task and partial-symptom both emit [0,1,0,0]

localAIPromptHints.adherent: The user is fully adhering to their care plan. No medication or task gaps detected.
localAIPromptHints.partial: The user is taking their medication but has missed some care plan tasks or has reported mild symptoms.
localAIPromptHints.lapsed: The user has missed medication doses. No symptom escalation — a gentle reminder is appropriate.
localAIPromptHints.concern: The user has missed medication doses AND has reported symptoms. A check-in with a care coordinator is warranted.

metadata.notes: Five sequences cover four output states: adherent (1), partial (2 sequences: partial-task and partial-symptom), lapsed (1), concern (1). Both partial sequences emit [0,1,0,0] so downstream consumers see a single 'partial' state.

sequences.name: Adherent: med=HIGH AND task=HIGH AND symptom=HIGH

metadata.description: Fires when all three compliance metrics are in their good range. Full care plan adherence — no action needed.

metadata.promptHint: The user is fully adhering to their care plan.

metadata.name: CAREKIT_ADHERENT_CHECK
metadata.description: element[0]: med_adherence HIGH (≥0.5); element[1]: task_completion HIGH; element[2]: symptom_ok HIGH; element[3]: reserved wildcard
Fires when medication is being taken but care plan tasks are falling behind. Symptom status is a wildcard — this is purely a task-adherence signal.

metadata.promptHint: The user is taking their medication but has missed some care plan tasks.

metadata.name: CAREKIT_PARTIAL_TASK_CHECK
metadata.description: element[0]: med HIGH; element[1]: task_completion LOW (<0.5, GTE split); element[2]: symptom wildcard; element[3]: reserved wildcard. Mutually exclusive with adherent (task is LOW here) and with lapsed/concern (med is HIGH).

sequences.name: Partial (symptom): med=HIGH AND task=HIGH AND symptom=LOW

metadata.description: Fires when medication is being taken and tasks are complete, but the user has reported symptoms. Strictly exclusive from partial-task (task is HIGH here).
Medication and tasks compliant but symptoms reported — partial
metadata.promptHint: The user is taking their medication and completing tasks, but has reported symptoms.

metadata.name: CAREKIT_PARTIAL_SYMPTOM_CHECK
metadata.description: element[0]: med HIGH; element[1]: task HIGH; element[2]: symptom_ok LOW (<0.5); element[3]: wildcard. Exclusive from partial-task (task=HIGH vs LOW).
Fires when medication has been missed but the user has not reported symptoms — a missed-dose window without clinical escalation. Task completion is a wildcard.
Missed medication, no symptom escalation — lapsed
metadata.promptHint: The user has missed medication doses. No symptom escalation detected — a gentle reminder is appropriate.

metadata.name: CAREKIT_LAPSED_CHECK
metadata.description: element[0]: med LOW (<0.5); element[1]: task wildcard; element[2]: symptom_ok HIGH (≥0.5); element[3]: wildcard. Mutually exclusive with adherent/partial (med is LOW here); exclusive with concern (symptom is HIGH here).
Fires when medication has been missed AND the user has reported symptoms — the highest-priority state. Task completion is a wildcard.

metadata.promptHint: The user has missed medication doses and has reported symptoms. A check-in with a care coordinator is warranted.

metadata.name: CAREKIT_CONCERN_CHECK
metadata.description: element[0]: med LOW; element[1]: task wildcard; element[2]: symptom_ok LOW (<0.5); element[3]: wildcard. Exclusive from lapsed (symptom=LOW here).

inputSequences.name: Full adherence — adherent
inputSequences.description: All three metrics in good range. carekit-adherent fires [1,0,0,0].

inputSequences.name: Tasks incomplete — partial
inputSequences.description: Medication taken but task completion is 20%. carekit-partial-task fires [0,1,0,0].

inputSequences.name: Symptoms reported — partial
inputSequences.description: Medication and tasks compliant but symptom check-in flagged. carekit-partial-symptom fires [0,1,0,0].

inputSequences.name: Missed doses, no symptoms — lapsed
inputSequences.description: Only 20% of doses taken but no symptom escalation. carekit-lapsed fires [0,0,1,0].

inputSequences.name: Missed doses with symptoms — concern
inputSequences.description: No doses taken and symptoms reported. carekit-concern fires [0,0,0,1].
